title: ConvertKit vs Brevo (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit vs Brevo. See pricing, features, ratings & find the best email marketing tool for your needs.
h1: ConvertKit vs Brevo: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: ConvertKit and Brevo are both popular freemium email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences with distinct strengths. ConvertKit focuses on creators and content makers, while Brevo offers a broader all-in-one CRM suite with multi-channel capabilities. This comparison will help you choose the right platform based on your specific needs and use case.

comparison_table.tool_a: Visual automations, landing pages, commerce features
comparison_table.tool_b: SMS, WhatsApp, live chat, CRM, transactional API

choose_a_if: You're a creator, blogger, or course seller looking for intuitive visual automation tools
You want built-in landing page and e-commerce features without third-party integrations
You prefer a platform specifically designed for content creators with a supportive community
choose_b_if: You need multi-channel marketing (email, SMS, WhatsApp, live chat) in one platform
You want unlimited free subscribers and prefer a more affordable paid tier
You require transactional email capabilities or advanced CRM features

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your use case. ConvertKit excels for creators with its intuitive interface and commerce features, while Brevo wins for businesses needing multi-channel marketing and comprehensive CRM functionality. ConvertKit has better Trustpilot reviews (3.2 vs 4.3), suggesting creator satisfaction varies more.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Brevo?
faq.a: Brevo offers significantly better value: its free tier supports 100,000 subscribers compared to ConvertKit's 1,000, and paid plans start at $25 vs $29. If you need multiple marketing channels, Brevo's all-in-one approach is more cost-effective than ConvertKit plus additional tools.

faq.q: Can I switch from Brevo to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support standard email list exports and imports. Most email marketing platforms allow CSV exports of your subscriber lists, making migration straightforward. However, you may lose some automation workflows and custom data fields during the transfer, so plan accordingly.
